title: Segment public API access tokens should not be disclosed
description: Segment is a customer data platform (CDP) that helps companies collect, clean, and activate their customer data across marketing, analytics, and data warehouse tools. The Segment Public API provides programmatic access to workspace configuration, allowing management of sources, destinations, tracking plans, schemas, and transformations. Public API access tokens authenticate API requests and grant extensive permissions including: reading and modifying tracking plans and event schemas, creating/updating/deleting sources and destinations, managing workspace settings and user permissions, accessing customer data pipeline configurations, viewing analytics data and event volumes, modifying data governance rules and privacy settings, managing integrations with downstream tools (Google Analytics, Amplitude, Mixpanel, data warehouses), and controlling data routing and transformation logic. Segment is widely used for: customer data infrastructure (unifying data from websites, mobile apps, servers, cloud tools), analytics and business intelligence (routing events to analytics platforms), marketing automation and personalization (sending customer data to marketing tools like Braze, Iterable, HubSpot), data warehousing (syncing customer data to Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift), and enabling a single source of truth for customer data across organizations. Exposing Public API tokens can lead to: unauthorized access to sensitive customer event data and user profiles, modification or deletion of critical data pipeline configurations causing data loss or corruption, injection of false or malicious event data into downstream systems, exposure of proprietary tracking plans revealing business logic and KPIs, disruption of analytics and marketing workflows affecting business operations, compliance violations (GDPR, CCPA) through unauthorized data access, exposure of API keys and credentials for connected third-party integrations, and potential account takeover leading to complete workspace compromise.
recommendation: Never hardcode Segment Public API access tokens in source code, configuration files, or version control. Immediately revoke the exposed token from Segment workspace settings (Settings → Access Management → Tokens), generate a new token with minimal required permissions (read-only if possible), and audit recent API activity for unauthorized access. Store tokens securely using environment variables (SEGMENT_PUBLIC_API_TOKEN), secret management systems (AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, Azure Key Vault, GCP Secret Manager), or encrypted configuration. Implement least privilege access by creating separate tokens for different use cases (analytics, data pipeline management, read-only monitoring). Enable IP whitelisting in Segment workspace settings if your use case allows static IP addresses. Rotate tokens regularly (every 90 days recommended) and monitor API usage logs for suspicious activity. Use Segment's audit logs to track configuration changes and detect unauthorized modifications to sources, destinations, or tracking plans.
impact: Critical security risk - Exposed Segment Public API tokens grant unauthorized access to modify customer data pipelines, tracking plans, and workspace configurations. Attackers can inject malicious event data, access sensitive customer profiles, delete critical sources/destinations, expose proprietary business logic through tracking plans, disrupt analytics workflows affecting business decisions, violate data privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA), and access credentials for connected third-party integrations. Financial impact from data breaches, compliance penalties, and operational disruption can be severe.

compliant.description: Using environment variables for Segment credentials
compliant.code:
import os
import requests

# Load Segment token from environment
SEGMENT_API_TOKEN = os.environ.get('SEGMENT_PUBLIC_API_TOKEN')

headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {SEGMENT_API_TOKEN}'}
response = requests.get('https://api.segmentapis.com/spaces', headers=headers)

checks.message: Segment Public API access token detected in source code. This token provides programmatic access to Segment's Public API and Config API, granting extensive permissions to manage workspace configuration, sources, destinations, tracking plans, schemas, and customer data pipelines. Exposing this token allows attackers to: access and modify tracking plans revealing proprietary business logic and KPIs, create/update/delete sources and destinations disrupting data flows to analytics and marketing tools, inject false or malicious event data into downstream systems (Google Analytics, Amplitude, Mixpanel, data warehouses), access customer event data and user profiles violating privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA), modify data governance rules and privacy settings compromising compliance, access API keys and credentials for connected third-party integrations, delete critical pipeline configurations causing data loss, modify transformation logic corrupting data quality, access workspace user permissions and settings, and gain insights into business operations through analytics data volumes and event patterns. For customer data platforms like Segment, exposure can lead to: complete compromise of customer data infrastructure affecting hundreds of downstream integrations, injection of tracking events that corrupt business analytics and ML models, exposure of sensitive user PII (emails, phone numbers, addresses, behavioral data), deletion of sources disrupting real-time data collection from websites and mobile apps, and unauthorized access to data warehouse sync jobs containing full customer databases. IMMEDIATELY revoke this token from Segment workspace at Settings → Access Management → Tokens, generate a new replacement token with minimal required permissions (use read-only tokens for monitoring, separate tokens for write operations), audit recent API activity in Segment's audit logs for unauthorized tracking plan changes, source/destination modifications, or suspicious data access patterns, review workspace configuration changes and revert any unauthorized modifications to sources, destinations, or tracking plans, check connected downstream integrations (analytics tools, marketing platforms, data warehouses) for signs of malicious event injection or data corruption, verify data governance rules and privacy settings haven't been tampered with, store tokens securely using environment variables (SEGMENT_PUBLIC_API_TOKEN), secret management systems (AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, HashiCorp Vault, GCP Secret Manager), or encrypted configuration (never commit to version control or client-side code), implement least privilege by creating separate tokens for different use cases (read-only monitoring, pipeline management, schema updates) and rotate tokens regularly (every 90 days recommended), enable IP whitelisting in workspace settings if your infrastructure uses static IPs, monitor audit logs continuously for configuration changes and set up alerts for sensitive operations (source/destination deletion, tracking plan modifications), use workspace access controls to limit token permissions to specific resources, implement rate limiting to prevent API abuse, and maintain an inventory of all API tokens with their purposes and rotation schedules.

Segment Public API access token detected (alphanumeric format). This token authenticates requests to Segment's Public API and Config API for workspace management. With this token, attackers can: modify tracking plans and event schemas exposing business intelligence, create/delete sources and destinations disrupting customer data flows, access sensitive customer event data and user profiles, inject malicious tracking events into downstream analytics and marketing tools, modify data governance and privacy settings, access connected integration credentials, and compromise the entire customer data platform infrastructure. Segment's CDP is critical infrastructure for many organizations, routing customer data to hundreds of downstream tools including Google Analytics, Amplitude, Mixpanel, Braze, HubSpot, Snowflake, BigQuery, and Redshift. Token exposure can lead to: complete data pipeline compromise affecting business analytics and decision-making, privacy regulation violations (GDPR, CCPA) through unauthorized customer data access, operational disruption as critical data flows are interrupted, exposure of proprietary tracking plans and business KPIs, and data corruption through malicious event injection. IMMEDIATELY revoke the exposed token from Segment workspace settings (Settings → Access Management → Tokens), generate a new token with minimal permissions, audit recent API calls in Segment's audit logs for unauthorized workspace configuration changes, tracking plan modifications, or suspicious data access, verify all sources and destinations are configured correctly and haven't been tampered with, check downstream systems for signs of malicious event data injection, review data governance rules and privacy settings for unauthorized changes, store tokens securely using environment variables (SEGMENT_PUBLIC_API_TOKEN) or secret management systems (AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, Azure Key Vault, GCP Secret Manager), implement least privilege access with separate read-only and write tokens for different use cases, rotate tokens every 90 days, enable IP whitelisting if available, monitor audit logs continuously for configuration changes, set up alerts for sensitive operations like source/destination deletion or tracking plan modifications, and maintain strict access controls on token distribution within your organization.
